choice: Resolve by source precedence, not wall-clock ingestion order: E*TRADE sync (authoritative position from custodian) > CSV import > manual entry, with most-recent as-of date breaking ties within the same source tier. Keep every row (provenance preserved); precedence only governs which row is treated as 'current'. Flag a conflict in the UI only when a manual entry is being overridden by a later sync, so a deliberate manual correction isn't silently clobbered.
rationale: 59576be9 stamped source precisely so it could drive conflict resolution, but left the rule unpinned. Ingestion-time-wins is fragile — a backfilled CSV or a delayed manual edit could overwrite a fresh authoritative sync. A custodian sync is the most trustworthy quantity for a real holding, so it should win by default; manual is the human escape hatch and should never be destroyed, only superseded-with-visibility.
